whatIs.p1: Most coding courses run in a browser — surrounded by notifications, social media, and infinite tabs.
whatIs.p1Bold: Kodo Forge takes a radically different approach.
whatIs.p2: It runs as a native application inside your terminal — the same tool professional developers use every single day to write and deploy software. No ads, no accounts, no tracking. Just you and the code.
whatIs.p3Bold: distraction-free environments dramatically improve learning retention
whatIs.p3End: . Kodo Forge combines this with techniques like spaced repetition (SM-2) and active recall — grounded in 12 learning theories from Sweller, Bjork, and Roediger — to help you truly master, not just consume, TypeScript, React, Angular, and full-stack web development.
whatIs.learnCycleSubtitle: Every lesson follows a proven 5-step methodology:

howItWorks.title: Start Learning in 3 Steps
howItWorks.subtitle: No sign-up. No credit card. No setup wizard. From download to learning in under 60 seconds.

steps.title: Download
steps.desc: Grab the single executable for Windows, macOS, or Linux. No Node.js, no Python, no admin rights. Just one 5 MB file.

steps.title: Launch
steps.desc: Double-click or run from shell. The TUI boots in 200 ms and loads the bundled courses — TypeScript is ready immediately.

steps.title: Learn & Master
steps.desc: Navigate with your keyboard. Read theory, run examples, solve exercises, take quizzes. Progress is saved locally — no cloud, no account.

items.a: Kodo Forge is a free, open-source Terminal User Interface (TUI) learning platform. It lets you learn TypeScript, Angular, React and Next.js directly inside your command line — with zero internet connection, zero dependencies, and zero distractions.

items.q: Is this really free — what's the catch?
items.a: No catch. MIT license, all 144 lessons across 4 courses included, no accounts, no telemetry, no "pro tier" locked behind a paywall. Kodo Forge is built by one developer who got tired of browser tutorials and wanted a tool they'd actually use. If you want to support it, star the GitHub repo or contribute a course.

items.q: Do I need an internet connection?
items.a: No. Kodo Forge runs entirely offline. Once you download the executable, everything works without any internet connection. Your progress is saved locally on your machine.

items.q: Which courses are actually available right now?
items.a: TypeScript Deep Learning (44 lessons, shipping now) is the complete course. Angular Mastery (40 lessons), React with TypeScript (40 lessons), and Next.js Production (20 lessons) are in active development — you can preview their curricula today and they unlock as they ship. All are free.

items.q: How is this different from Udemy, Codecademy, or freeCodeCamp?
items.a: Three concrete differences. (1) It runs in your terminal, not a browser — no notifications, no autoplay videos, no social features to scroll. (2) Spaced repetition is native, not bolted on: every concept goes into a review queue automatically. (3) Adaptive depth: you pick summary, standard, or deep-dive for each section, so experts don't slog through basics. Udemy sells courses; Kodo Forge teaches retention.

items.q: Can I create my own courses?
items.a: Absolutely. Kodo Forge is a data-driven engine. Courses are just folders of Markdown files. You can create courses on any topic — not just programming. See the docs for the content authoring guide.
Kodo Forge provides standalone executables for Windows (x64), macOS (Apple Silicon), and Linux (x64). No installation required — just download and run.

docsPage.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Kodo Forge — from getting started to creating your own terminal courses.
docsPage.openSourceDesc: Contribute, fork, or just study the code. The entire engine is written in clean, well-documented TypeScript.

createPage.title: Author Your Own
createPage.subtitle: Kodo Forge isn't just a learning platform — it's an open-source, data-driven reading engine. Create interactive terminal courses using nothing but Markdown files.
createPage.howItWorksP1: The Kodo Forge engine knows absolutely nothing about TypeScript or React. It purely reads a platform.json file and parses the folders you define inside it.
createPage.howItWorksP2: A course is simply a folder on your computer. Inside that folder, you create numbered subfolders for your lessons (e.g. 01-foundations/). Inside those are your Markdown files.
createPage.howItWorksP3: When you launch Kodo Forge, the engine compiles your Markdown into a highly optimized terminal experience — complete with syntax highlighting, annotated code, adaptive depth modes, and auto-generated quizzes.
createPage.writeMarkdownDesc: No proprietary builders. Just write standard GitHub Flavored Markdown. Code fences, bold, italic, tables, and blockquotes all supported.
createPage.terminalNativeDesc: Kodo Forge automatically parses your files into a beautiful TUI context, turning blockquotes into callout boxes and headings into semantic dividers.
createPage.annotatedCodeDesc: Add `[annotated]` to your code fences, and the engine splits the view to show your inline `// comments` as beautiful side-by-side explanations.
createPage.aiDesc: A deterministic AI workflow instruction set to rapidly prototype courses. Give the prompt to Claude, Gemini, or ChatGPT, and they generate fully Kodo-Forge-compatible directory structures and Markdown files for any topic you want to teach.
createPage.metaDescription: Author your own terminal courses with Kodo Forge. Write Markdown, define a folder structure, and the engine renders a beautiful TUI learning experience.

typescript.tagline: From basics to type-level programming — 44 lessons, 5 phases
typescript.description: The most comprehensive TypeScript course you'll find in a terminal. 44 lessons, 5 phases, from primitives to the Compiler API.
typescript.longDescription:
This isn't another TypeScript tutorial that stops at interfaces and generics. It's a 44-lesson, 82-hour deep dive that takes you from your first type annotation to writing your own type-level programs and manipulating the Compiler AST.

Every lesson follows the proven LEARN cycle: read theory enriched with diagrams and real-world analogies, explore runnable code examples annotated line-by-line, apply your knowledge through progressively harder exercises, reflect via interactive terminal quizzes, and retain everything with compact cheatsheets and spaced repetition.

What sets this apart from every other TypeScript resource: the built-in spaced repetition engine tracks every concept you learn and schedules reviews at scientifically optimal intervals — right before you'd forget. No other course does this. You build genuine, permanent knowledge instead of a fleeting tutorial high.

The curriculum spans everything a professional needs: primitive types, generics, mapped and conditional types, template literals, discriminated unions, branded types, decorators, the full Compiler API, and type-level programming. Phase 5 pushes into expert territory with TypeScript security patterns, fully typed RxJS pipelines, and 15+ GoF/SOLID design patterns implemented type-safely.
typescript.heroQuote: The TypeScript course that remembers what you forget.
targetAudience: JavaScript developers who want to truly understand TypeScript
Self-learners looking for a structured learning path
Developers preparing for Angular, React, or Next.js
Professionals who want to deepen their type system knowledge
whatYouLearn: All primitive types and when to use annotations vs. inference
Generics: from basics to complex higher-order types
Master mapped types, conditional types, and template literal types
Discriminated unions and exhaustive pattern matching
Branded/nominal types for type-safe IDs and opaque types
Result<T,E> patterns for type-safe error handling
The Compiler API and AST manipulation
Type-level programming — computing with the type system
15+ design patterns (GoF, SOLID, Builder, State Machine) implemented type-safely
TypeScript security: recognizing and avoiding dangerous patterns

didacticHighlights.title: Spaced Repetition
didacticHighlights.description: Every concept is automatically added to your review system. The algorithm schedules reviews optimally — right before you'd forget.

didacticHighlights.title: Adaptive Reading Depth
didacticHighlights.description: Every section offers 3 modes: summary, standard, and deep-dive. You decide how deep you want to go.

didacticHighlights.title: Interactive Quizzes
didacticHighlights.description: At the end of every lesson: multiple choice, code gaps, and concept questions — right in the terminal.

didacticHighlights.title: Annotated Code Blocks
didacticHighlights.description: Code examples with line-by-line annotations that explain what each line does and why.

didacticHighlights.title: Review Challenges
didacticHighlights.description: Every 10 lessons: a phase review that combines everything you've learned and tests your understanding.

didacticHighlights.title: Mermaid Diagrams
didacticHighlights.description: Complex relationships are displayed as visual diagrams right in the terminal.
